segments.text: As kids, many of us wanted to be an
astronaut. Now, ISRO is making sure
those dreams actually come true. First,
follow full disclosure and let me show
you what's happening. ISRO launched UIA
2026 young scientist program designed to
introduce class 9 students to space
science technology and applications. The
program runs from May 11th to May 22nd,
2026. [music]
two weeks fully residential at major
ISRO centers including Vikram Sarabhai
Space Center, Satish Dhawan Space Center
and URAO satellite center. Students
attend expert lectures, facility visits,
hands-on demonstrations and interact
with ISRO scientists working on India
space missions. [music]
Eligibility is straightforward. You must
be studying in class 9th as of January
1st, 2026 in any school within India. It
will give you two weeks inside ISRO
facilities getting to watch how
satellites are built, how missions are
planned and how India space technology
actually works. Applications open
February 27, 2026 and deadline is March
